name: Chayotes with Corn and Chiles
recipeIngredient: 2 Fresh Anaheim or poblano chiles, charred, peeled, and diced
1 cup Frozen or fresh corn kernels (cut from 2 ears of corn)
1/2 tsp Red chile powder, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, sauté the chopped red onion in olive oil over medium heat until softened.
2. Peel the chayotes (chayote has thin skin and can be easily peeled with a swivel-blade peeler), cut each into halves, scoop out and discard the seed, then dice the flesh. Add to the saucepan.
3. Add the diced chiles, corn kernels, salt, pepper, and evaporated milk. Stir to combine.
4. Cover and simmer gently for about 15 minutes, or until the chayotes are tender.
5. Stir in the grated Parmesan cheese and cook for a few seconds until the sauce thickens.
6. Transfer the mixture to a serving bowl and sprinkle with grated sharp cheddar cheese and red chile powder.
7. Serve as a vegetarian entree or alongside grilled meats.
